title: Resinous Springy American Garlic Hash
description: A hearty, comforting breakfast dish featuring a sticky, slightly sweet confit made with garlic and tomatoes, mixed with a crispy, slightly charred hash of diced vegetables and potatoes. This Shojin-friendly recipe is perfect for a weekend brunch or a cozy dinner.
instructions: Toss the diced potatoes with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and pepper on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
In a skillet, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the diced red onion, yellow bell pepper, and halved tomatoes to the skillet and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the roasted potatoes and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the mixture is well combined.
Use a spatula to press the mixture down into the skillet, creating a crispy crust on the bottom.
Serve the hash hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
